name: Pesto Risotto
recipeInstructions:
- Chicken broth (4 cups)
- Dry white wine (1 cup)
- Olive oil (2 tbsp)
- Arborio rice (1 1/2 cups)
- Minced garlic cloves (2)
- Salt (optional, 1/4 tsp)
1. Prepare pesto sauce: Process basil leaves, cheese, parsley sprigs, pine nuts, and minced garlic in a food processor fitted with a chopping blade until a smooth paste forms. Gradually add olive oil and process until well blended. Set aside.
2. Heat broth and wine in a 2-quart saucepan just to boiling. Remove from heat.
3. In a large skillet, heat olive oil over medium heat. Add rice and garlic; sauté, stirring constantly, for 3 minutes or until the rice starts to brown.
4. Gradually add hot broth mixture (about 1/2 cup at a time) into the skillet, stirring constantly, until all liquid is absorbed and the rice is creamy but slightly al dente (about 20 to 25 minutes). If desired, season with salt to taste.
5. Spoon risotto onto serving dish. Arrange pesto sauce in a spiral pattern over the risotto. Top with basil sprig and serve immediately.
